label: Hospitals Damaged
source: Iranian Deputy Health Min. / WHO / OCHA
detail: 31 major hospitals damaged in Iran, 9 rendered non-operational per Day 7 reports, 69+ schools damaged. WHO verified 18 attacks on healthcare in Iran (8 health worker deaths) and 25 attacks on healthcare in Lebanon (16 deaths, 29 injuries) as of Mar 11. 77 healthcare facilities affected per Red Crescent. 12 medical workers killed in strike on Hezbollah-run health center Day 22.

label: Schools Destroyed
source: Iranian Red Crescent / AP / NYT / Amnesty
detail: Girls' school struck by US Tomahawk cruise missile adjacent to IRGC naval compound. 165-180 killed (mostly girls aged 7-12). NYT (Mar 11): preliminary investigation determined US responsible due to outdated DIA targeting coordinates — school was part of IRGC compound until ~2013-2016. Amnesty (Mar 16) confirmed 'serious breach of international humanitarian law.' UN investigation ongoing. Trump: 'I don't know about that.'

label: Power Plants Hit
detail: Grid disruptions across western Iran. Isfahan, Kermanshah, Ahvaz power infrastructure degraded. Civilian blackouts reported in multiple provinces.

label: Oil/Gas Facilities Hit
note: Kharg, Abadan, South Pars + Ras Laffan (Qatar) + SAMREF (Saudi) + Kuwait refineries
source: DoD / Bloomberg / QatarEnergy / Reuters / satellite imagery
detail: Iran's energy infrastructure: Kharg Island terminal destroyed. South Pars gas field Phases 3/4/5/6 struck Mar 18 — 730M cm³/day field offline. Ras Laffan LNG Trains 4+6 damaged (12.8 MTPA, 3-5yr repair, $20B/yr loss). Pearl GTL offline 1+ year. SAMREF refinery at Yanbu struck. Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi (2 drone waves Mar 19-20) and Mina Abdullah struck. Haifa BAZAN refinery struck. Bahrain Bapco Sitra under force majeure. Ruwais (UAE, 922k bpd) remains shut. Panama Canal at max capacity (36-38/day) due to LNG rerouting.

label: Nuclear Facilities Struck
note: Fordow, Isfahan UCF, Natanz (3x since Jun 2025), Bushehr perimeter
detail: Fordow enrichment facility struck Day 1 with B-2/GBU-57 MOPs. Isfahan UCF struck Day 1. Natanz Shahid Ahmadi-Roshan Enrichment Complex struck Day 22 with bunker-buster bombs (likely GBU-57 MOPs and/or GBU-72). IAEA: no radiation increase. DG Grossi: 'military restraint to avoid nuclear accident.' Russia condemned. Israel denied involvement. Entrance buildings of underground Fuel Enrichment Plant damaged. Bulk of Iran's enriched uranium (~440 kg) believed at Isfahan, not Natanz. Third Natanz strike since June 2025. Bushehr NPP perimeter hit Day 19 — ~480 Russians on site.

label: Airports Damaged
detail: Mehrabad (Tehran), Shiraz TAB 6, Tabriz TAB 2 runways cratered in first wave. Dubai International Airport struck by drone Mar 16. Regional airspace closures affect 24,000+ commercial flights.

label: Bridges Destroyed
detail: Road and rail bridges in Kermanshah, Lorestan, and Khuzestan provinces destroyed to degrade IRGC logistics and prevent resupply to missile launch sites.
